task_completion.description: Open the correct Jupyter notebook and load the UrbanSim H5 dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Jupyter Notebook open with the title/path 'fire_resource_assessment.ipynb'. Inside the notebook, look for code importing pandas and reading the H5 file from '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5'.

task_completion.description: Implement the logic to identify 'High-Demand Buildings' and handle NaNs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code cells containing conditions for high-demand buildings: 'residential_units >= 30', 'non_residential_sqft >= 50000', and 'stories >= 5'. Also look for code handling NaNs, such as '.fillna(0)'.

task_completion.description: Aggregate data by zone_id and calculate the fire_resource_score.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code using '.groupby('zone_id')' and calculating counts/sums. Crucially, look for the scoring formula: multiplying high demand buildings by 5 and adding total residential units in high demand multiplied by 0.1.

task_completion.description: Filter, sort, and export the dataframe to the expected CSV path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code filtering out zones with 0 high-demand buildings, sorting by score descending, and a '.to_csv()' command saving to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/fire_resource_demand.csv'.

task_completion.description: Calculate summary statistics and export to the expected JSON path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code calculating the citywide total of high-demand buildings, the max score zone_id, and the average score of the top 10 zones, followed by code writing a JSON file to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/safety_summary.json'.

task_completion.description: Generate and save a horizontal bar chart of the top 20 zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for plotting code (e.g., '.plot.barh()' or 'plt.barh()') for the top 20 zones, and a command like 'plt.savefig()' saving the image to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/top_20_fire_demand_zones.png'.

task_completion.description: Execute all notebook cells to produce outputs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the left side of the notebook cells; they should have execution numbers (e.g., 'In [1]:', 'In [2]:') rather than empty brackets 'In [ ]:'. Output dataframes, print statements, or the inline bar chart should be visible below the cells.

integrity.description: The agent computed the results rather than hardcoding the expected answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the code cells to ensure the agent is using pandas operations to derive the 3,123 high-demand buildings and other metrics, rather than just typing the final numbers into a dictionary or JSON string.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jupyter Notebook interface to write and run code.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the screenshots show the Jupyter Notebook web interface (or VS Code notebook editor) with active cell executions, rather than just a plain text editor without execution capabilities.

integrity.description: The plot was generated by the code and not copied from an external source.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the inline display of the matplotlib/seaborn plot within the notebook output cell, matching the code written above it.
